handoff_goal: Specify a globally available internal agent tool capability that lets the agent inspect, explain, validate, create, update, duplicate/copy, and delete workflow templates through conversation while preserving existing Workflows validation, persistence, field guidance, permission, and session-snapshot safety rules.
coverage_status: live-evidence-backed; project cognition stale/blocked and used only as advisory

current_project_roles.scope: Built-in agent tools, workflow template registry/API, workflow template persistence, workflow runtime/session behavior, workflow tool policy, and desktop workflow management surfaces.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: User request plus live repository reads.
current_project_roles.notes: The user is extending the current repository's Workflows feature.

target_project_roles.scope: Add an internal tool capability so agents can author and mutate workflow templates from ordinary chat.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: User request plus live repository reads.
target_project_roles.notes: No cross-project transfer or external target was requested.

target_paths_still_to_verify: Exact new tool file/module placement under src/tools.
Whether desktop-launched sessions need a desktop-server-backed authoring path or can use direct registry-service writes.
Whether field guide content should be generated from shared schema metadata, validator annotations, or a static tested guide colocated with validation.
implementation_target.cognition_authority_rule: Current project cognition cannot prove implementation facts. Downstream work must use live repository reads for final code claims.

source_evidence.claim: The desired capability is direct conversational workflow authoring through agent tools.
Valid user-template create/update should write directly without mandatory preview.
The tool should be globally available in ordinary chat, with active workflow phase-policy gating.
Existing user templates update in place; builtin templates copy to a user-owned template before editing.
Delete is first-scope but only for uniquely identified user templates and must be destructive.
The agent needs explicit field guidance/schema help before authoring workflows.
Built-in tools are assembled centrally and workflow-scoped tools are added separately.
Tool contract supports schema, validation, permission, read-only/destructive metadata, and transcript rendering hooks.
Current workflow-specific tool submits phase completion only; it does not mutate workflow templates.
Registry validates templates, preserves unknown fields, writes user workflow config, rejects builtin ID shadowing, and defines the current required field contract.
Manual API already supports list/detail/validate/create/update/delete/duplicate/import/export and protects builtin templates from direct mutation.
Workflow template/session types include template snapshots, phase definitions, and lifecycle/source-template statuses.
Workflow sessions use template snapshots, supporting the non-goal of in-place session mutation.
Existing phase tool policy denies implementation tools in non-implementation phases; new global tool exposure must respect this policy.
Desktop API client already wraps workflow template API operations.
Manual Settings UI supports workflow template management and maps editor fields into the template draft contract.
Existing tests cover API validation, builtin readonly behavior, create/update/delete/duplicate/import/export, conflict handling, and no-write validation.

soft.unknown: Whether desktop-launched sessions require a desktop-server-backed tool path in addition to direct registry-service writes.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if one runtime path cannot safely access the registry service or preserve desktop API behavior.

soft.unknown: Whether the field guide is generated from shared schema metadata, validator annotations, or a static tested guide colocated with validation.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if the proposed guide can drift from validation or cannot be tested deterministically.

settled_decisions: Implement a globally available internal agent workflow authoring tool using Option A from technical-options.md.
Include read-only operations for listing/inspecting templates and retrieving a field authoring guide/schema help.
Include validating and mutating operations for user templates: create, update, duplicate/copy, and delete.
Mutating valid user-template create/update operations persist directly. There is no mandatory preview step.
Delete is first-scope, destructive, and only valid for uniquely identified user templates.
Builtin templates such as agent-development remain read-only. Edit requests copy the builtin to a user template and then modify that copy.
Builtin copy defaults: id <builtin-id>-custom, then -2, -3, etc. on conflicts; display name <Builtin Name> Custom unless user intent gives a clearer name.
Update/delete must re-read the current template and reject stale writes when the current version differs from the agent's original basis.
Active/running/historical workflow sessions keep their template snapshots; template edits affect future sessions only.
Global availability must respect active workflow phase action policy and permission/destructive metadata.
Validation diagnostics and tool results must be actionable and auditable in the transcript.
assumptions_to_preserve: Settings Workflows already provides manual template management and should remain compatible.
The tool should reuse or share registry/API validation semantics rather than inventing a separate schema.
Project cognition is stale/blocked and must not be used as source authority for implementation facts.
conflicts_requiring_return_to_sp_discuss: Mandatory preview-only persistence for normal valid user-template create/update.
Direct builtin mutation or builtin ID shadowing.
Deleting builtin templates or deleting ambiguous targets.
Mutating active/historical workflow session snapshots as a side effect of template edits.
Writing outside the workflow template config or touching protected user state.
A global authoring tool that bypasses phase action policy.
A field guide that is not discoverable by the agent or can drift from validation.
Unsupported workflow shapes without an explicit new product decision.

capability_map.required_behavior: Read-only field guide covering template/phase fields, required relationships, allowed values, examples, unsupported shapes, and quality heuristics.
Return builtin and user templates plus invalid-template diagnostics without writing.
Return one template's field contract, phase structure, source, editability, and validation state.
Validate candidate payloads using the same registry/API semantics and return issue paths/codes/messages without writing.
Create a new user template after validation and current ID conflict check.
Update an existing user template after validation, source/id checks, and stale-write comparison.
Create a non-conflicting user copy, especially for builtin edit requests.
Delete only uniquely identified user templates; mark destructive; reject builtin or ambiguous targets.

recommended_sequence: Specify tool contract, operation schema, permission/read-only/destructive metadata, and transcript result shape.
Specify shared validation/write semantics with WorkflowTemplateRegistryService and /api/workflows/templates.
Specify read-only field guide/schema help and how it stays aligned with validation.
Specify create/update/duplicate/delete behavior, including builtin copy-then-edit, stale-write rejection, ID conflicts, and delete ambiguity.
Specify global registration and active workflow phase-policy gating.

Specify tests: tool unit tests, registry/API parity tests, field-guide output tests, stale-write/delete safety tests, builtin copy tests, phase-policy gating tests, and desktop API/client tests only if desktop behavior changes.
dependencies: Existing registry validation and write semantics.
Existing workflow template API behavior and tests.
deferred_scope: Supporting parallel, nested, branching, or looping workflows.
Direct builtin template mutation or deletion.
In-place migration of active workflow sessions after a template edit.
Source-code edits as part of a workflow-template authoring operation.
New Settings UI redesign beyond compatibility/refetch behavior.
reopen_conditions: Mandatory preview-only persistence for normal valid user-template create/update.
Direct builtin mutation or builtin ID shadowing.
Deleting builtin templates or deleting ambiguous targets.
Mutating active/historical workflow session snapshots as a side effect of template edits.
Writing outside the workflow template config or touching protected user state.
A global authoring tool that bypasses phase action policy.
A field guide that is not discoverable by the agent or can drift from validation.
Unsupported workflow shapes without an explicit new product decision.

confirmed_ui_decisions: Existing Settings Workflows should show agent-created or agent-updated templates after reload/refetch.
No new first-class UI design is required for this handoff.
deferred_ui_decisions: If downstream implementation changes Settings UI feedback, handle it as compatibility behavior in specification/plan.
interaction_expectations: Tool results should be readable in chat and include operation outcome, affected template IDs/source, validation issues, before/after summary, and suggested next action.
state_requirements: No preview UI is required before valid user-template writes.
Invalid/rejected/stale/permission-denied operations must leave persisted config unchanged.
ui_discussion.accessibility_expectations: not_applicable for the internal tool; preserve existing Settings UI expectations if touched.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Provide an internal agent tool capability for conversational workflow template authoring and optimization.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Preserve as the primary feature goal.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if the feature becomes manual UI-only or draft-only.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: User can describe a new workflow and phases; the agent fills required fields, validates, persists, and Settings Workflows can show it after refetch.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if create cannot persist valid user templates.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: User can critique an existing workflow and the agent can analyze and apply a validated update or safe copy.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify update and builtin copy-then-edit behavior.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if builtin edits are direct mutations or user-template updates are not supported.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: First scope includes list, inspect, field guide, validate, create, update, duplicate/copy, and delete.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if delete or field guide is dropped without explicit user decision.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Valid user-template create/update writes directly without a mandatory preview step.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify direct validated writes and no-write failures.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if mandatory preview-only flow is reintroduced.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Tool is globally available in ordinary chat but must respect active workflow phase action policy.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if global availability bypasses workflow phase restrictions.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Builtin templates are read-only mutation targets; builtin edit requests create and modify a user-owned copy.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify copy-then-edit and readonly builtin enforcement.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if direct builtin mutation or builtin ID shadowing is proposed.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Update/delete must re-read current template and reject stale writes when it differs from the agent's basis.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if update/delete writes from stale list state.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Delete is destructive, first-scope, and only valid for uniquely identified user templates.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if builtin or ambiguous deletes are allowed.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Builtin copy IDs default to <builtin-id>-custom with numeric suffixes; names default to <Builtin Name> Custom unless user intent supplies clearer naming.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if copied builtin templates receive confusing IDs/names or collide.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Agent must have a discoverable schema-aligned field guide before authoring or repairing templates.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify read-only guide operation and validation alignment.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if downstream relies on undocumented model knowledge or stale field docs.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Do not mutate protected non-workflow user state such as shared settings, transcripts, providers, MCP config, OAuth tokens, skills, plugin state, adapters, or team/session records.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if implementation writes outside cc-jiangxia workflow template config.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Do not support parallel, nested, branching, or loop workflows in first scope.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if unsupported workflow shapes are added without explicit scope expansion.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Do not mutate active or historical workflow session snapshots when templates change.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if template edit migrates active session state.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Reuse or share existing registry/API validation and persistence semantics.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify validation parity and unknown-field preservation.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if a separate validator drifts from API behavior.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Live source evidence, not stale/blocked project cognition, proves current project facts.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if downstream relies on cognition as authority without live reads.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Option A is selected over preview/commit or manual-only approaches.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if Option B/C replaces Option A without user confirmation.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Invalid, risky, stale, ambiguous, or permission-denied operations must not write and must return actionable diagnostics.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if failures can partially write or return generic errors only.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Settings Workflows remains compatible and can display agent-authored templates after reload/refetch.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify compatibility with manual management surface.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if agent writes use a separate store invisible to Settings.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Tool results must make persistence state auditable in the transcript.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Specify result shape with operation, affected IDs, validation issues, before/after summary, and next action.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if tool returns opaque success/failure.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Decide whether desktop-launched sessions need a desktop-server-backed path in addition to direct registry writes.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Resolve during plan; not a specification blocker.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if runtime access path cannot preserve registry/API semantics.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Decide how the field guide stays aligned with validation: shared schema metadata, validator annotations, or a static tested guide.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Resolve during plan; not a specification blocker.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if the selected guide design can drift from validation.

senior_consequence_analysis.trigger_reason: Agent-authored workflow changes can mutate user workflow templates, alter future workflow execution semantics, affect active or resumed workflow sessions, interact with manual Settings edits, and change which tools/actions are allowed inside phases.
affected_object_map: User workflow config: ~/.claude/cc-jiangxia/workflows.json or active CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR equivalent.
User workflow templates and their phase contracts.
Workflow template registry cache and write path.
Desktop Settings Workflows UI and API client.
Built-in agent tool pool, permissions, destructive metadata, and transcript rendering.
Workflow session template snapshots and source-template status recovery.

state_behavior_matrix.required_behavior: Listing remains valid with builtin templates; validated user-template mutation may create workflow config.
Agent writes are blocked; diagnostics returned; invalid config not overwritten.
Read-only response; no workflow config mutation.
May exist transiently; valid create/update writes when tool is called.
No write; return issue codes, paths, messages, and repair guidance.
Persist after validation and current ID conflict check.
Source/id must match; re-read basis; preserve unknown fields where registry does.
Only uniquely identified source:user; destructive; no builtin or ambiguous delete.
Copy builtin to non-conflicting user template then edit copy.
Reject or propose non-conflicting rename; never silently overwrite.
Keep template snapshot stable; edits affect future sessions only.
Update/delete reject stale writes; create checks current ID conflicts.

dependency_impact_table.required_handling: Reuse/share validation and write semantics; preserve unknown fields.
Keep manual API and internal tool behavior aligned.
Register the tool globally with deliberate read-only/write/destructive permission behavior.
Preserve snapshot behavior and future-session-only template effect.
Ensure agent-authored templates remain visible after list refetch.
Gate authoring writes when active phase policy forbids them.
Co-locate/generate/test guide so it does not drift from validation.
Add same-area server/tool coverage and desktop/API tests if changed.

recovery_and_validation_contract: Invalid candidate, permission denial, stale conflict, ambiguous delete, builtin delete, or validation failure must leave persisted config unchanged.
Successful writes must invalidate registry cache.
Failed writes should report operation, template id/source, issue paths/codes/messages, and whether persistence happened.
Update/delete stale conflicts should instruct the agent to re-read/re-analyze before retry.
Field guide must be deterministic and testable.

Verification should include tool unit tests, registry/API parity tests, field-guide tests, destructive delete tests, stale-write tests, builtin copy tests, phase-policy gating tests, and desktop tests if the desktop path changes.

coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Reopen if update/delete writes without comparing current template to original basis.
Reopen if copied builtin IDs/names collide or are confusing.
Reopen if the chosen runtime path cannot preserve registry/API semantics.
Reopen if field guide can drift from validation.

consequence_obligations.claim: Agent-authored valid user-template create/update mutations should persist directly without a mandatory preview step, while validation failure or tool rejection leaves config unchanged.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Downstream design reintroduces mandatory preview-only persistence.

consequence_obligations.claim: Agent writes must preserve user-owned workflow config fields and must not mutate protected non-workflow config surfaces.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Proposed implementation writes outside the cc-jiangxia workflow template config.

consequence_obligations.claim: The tool must share validation semantics with the existing template registry/API and block invalid writes.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: A separate validator diverges from existing API behavior.

consequence_obligations.claim: Builtin templates remain read-only mutation targets; modifying a builtin requires creating a non-conflicting user-owned copy and applying changes to that copy.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Downstream design attempts direct builtin mutation or builtin ID shadowing.

consequence_obligations.claim: Template edits must not mutate active or historical workflow session snapshots.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Design updates active session state as a side effect of template update.

consequence_obligations.claim: Tool results must make persistence state auditable in the transcript.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Tool returns only generic success/failure without operation and affected-template metadata.

consequence_obligations.claim: Concurrent manual and agent edits require write-before-read conflict protection: update/delete re-read the current template and reject stale writes when it differs from the agent's original basis; create checks current ID conflicts.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Update/delete path writes based on stale list state without detection.

consequence_obligations.claim: The workflow authoring tool should be globally available in ordinary chat, but must align with active workflow phase action policy.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Tool is globally available with no phase/permission gating decision.

consequence_obligations.claim: Delete is in first scope only for uniquely identified user templates and must be marked destructive; builtin or ambiguous deletes must be rejected or clarified before any write.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Downstream design allows builtin deletion, ambiguous target deletion, or non-destructive delete execution.

consequence_obligations.claim: Builtin copy-then-edit default naming must use <builtin-id>-custom with conflict suffixes and <Builtin Name> Custom unless the user's requested purpose/name produces a clearer non-conflicting copy.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Copied builtin templates receive confusing IDs/names or collide with existing user templates.

consequence_obligations.claim: The agent must have a discoverable workflow template field guide before authoring or repairing templates, and it must stay aligned with registry/API validation.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Downstream design relies on undocumented model knowledge or stale field docs.

self_review_summary: Context boundary is locked to F:\github\cc-jiangxia.
Handoff goal is concrete and unified.
Must-Preserve Ledger includes goal, scope, non-goals, scenarios, decisions, references, selected tradeoff, and soft downstream questions.
Senior consequence obligations CA-001 through CA-011 are preserved.
Markdown and JSON companion must remain synchronized before user confirmation.
